title: Quiz: Channel Simulation and Resource Theory
description: Test your understanding of channel simulation and resource theories

questions.question: Channel simulation asks:

options.text: How to measure a channel's capacity
How to implement one channel using another plus resources
How to decompose a channel into gates

questions.explanation: Channel simulation studies how to implement one quantum channel using another channel plus additional resources like shared entanglement or classical communication.

questions.question: Teleportation-based channel simulation requires:
questions.explanation: Teleportation-based simulation requires shared entanglement (1 ebit per qubit) and classical communication (2 bits per qubit) to teleport the input, then local channel application.

questions.question: Entanglement-breaking channels have zero entanglement cost for simulation.
questions.explanation: True. EB channels can be simulated using only classical communication and local operations - no shared entanglement is needed.

questions.question: The quantum reverse Shannon theorem relates channel simulation to:
questions.explanation: The quantum reverse Shannon theorem shows that channel simulation and channel coding are dual problems - simulation requires resources at the capacity rate.

questions.question: The entanglement cost E_C(N) is bounded above by:

options.text: The entanglement of formation of the Choi state

questions.explanation: E_C(N) <= E_F(rho_N) where rho_N is the Choi state of the channel and E_F is the entanglement of formation.

questions.question: In the resource theory of channels, free channels typically include:
questions.explanation: Free channels in typical resource theories include LOCC (local operations + classical communication) and entanglement-breaking channels.

questions.question: Port-based teleportation achieves perfect fidelity with finite resources.
questions.explanation: False. Port-based teleportation achieves perfect fidelity only in the limit of infinitely many ports. With finite ports, there is always some error.

questions.question: A channel monotone satisfies:

options.text: f(N) >= f(M) if N can simulate M

questions.explanation: A monotone f satisfies f(N) >= f(M) when N dominates M (N can simulate M with free operations). Quantum capacity is an example.
